title: The "Object.finalize()" method should not be overridden
description: Overriding the finalize() method can lead to unpredictable behavior, as the garbage collector calls finalize() on an object before reclaiming its storage. However, this method may not be called as soon as the object's references are removed, leading to a potential delay. This can cause issues with resource release and program state updates. Additionally, the JVM might terminate without ever calling this method on a specific object, leading to unexpected program states. Uncaught exceptions will also be ignored inside this method, making it harder to detect issues. Finalizer methods can also be invoked concurrently, posing difficulty in maintaining program invariants.
recommendation: Avoid overriding the Object.finalize() method. If necessary, it's allowed to override it as a final method with an empty body to prevent the finalizer attack.
impact: Relying on the finalize() method to perform important operations can lead to unpredictable program state, uncaught exceptions being ignored, and difficulty in maintaining program invariants.

checks.message: Avoid overriding the Object.finalize() method. If necessary, it's allowed to override it as a final method with an empty body to prevent the finalizer attack.
